extra.message: User-controlled post_id is concatenated directly into SQL query string passed to conn.execute(). The weak regex filter (lines 67-68) only checks for digits at the start of the string, but the concatenation still enables SQL injection if the regex is bypassed or if the filter logic fails. The post_id parameter originates from the URL route.
Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'qwerty12345'. This key is used for signing session cookies and CSRF tokens. An attacker who knows this key can forge valid session cookies to impersonate any user.
Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as '55555'. This key is used for signing session cookies and CSRF tokens. An attacker who knows this key can forge valid session cookies to impersonate any user.

Path traversal vulnerability: user-controlled self.path is concatenated directly to the working directory path (line 14) and used to read and serve arbitrary files (line 22). The regex filter on line 11 uses re.match() which only anchors at the start of the string, making it bypassable with paths like '/assets/../../../etc/passwd'. Additionally, the regex pattern only checks for literal '../../', missing other traversal patterns like '..\' or URL-encoded variants. This allows an attacker to read any file on the filesystem.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF): user-supplied URL from request.args.get('url') is fetched via urllib.request.urlopen() after only checking that it starts with 'https://insecurewebapp.com'. This check is trivially bypassable — an attacker can use URLs like 'https://insecurewebapp.com.attacker.com' or 'https://insecurewebapp.com@attacker.com' to make the server fetch arbitrary external or internal resources.

Length extension attack vulnerability: the signature is computed as hashlib.sha256(secret + payment_information) instead of using HMAC. SHA-256 is vulnerable to length extension attacks when used in this secret-prefix construction. An attacker who knows the original message and signature can append arbitrary data and compute a valid signature without knowing the secret, allowing forgery of payment information.

cwe: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

extra.message: Length extension attack vulnerability: the signature is computed as hashlib.sha256(secret + payment_information) instead of using HMAC. SHA-256 is vulnerable to length extension attacks when used in this secret-prefix construction. An attacker who knows the original message and signature can append arbitrary data and compute a valid signature without knowing the secret, allowing forgery of payment information.

cwe: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

extra.message: SSL certificate hostname verification is explicitly disabled via context.check_hostname = False. This makes the TLS connection vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks, as any certificate presented by the server will be accepted regardless of whether it matches the expected hostname.

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS): the username value derived from a user-supplied cookie (decrypted at line 26) is inserted directly into HTML output via string formatting without any HTML escaping. If an attacker can control the encrypted cookie value (e.g., by registering with a malicious username that the server later encrypts into a session cookie), the payload will execute in the browser of any user whose session cookie is decrypted and rendered.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
